label: PASS
rationale: Locking_Range1 is preconfigured with LockOnReset Power Cycle, and reset_types value 0 is Power Cycle. The Admin Get ACE covers LockOnReset, so returning column 9 as [0] is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: No prior successful Locking Set changes column 9, so Locking_Range1 remains preconfigured as Power Cycle ([0]). The target returned [1], Hardware, which is neither the preconfigured value nor an Opal-required standalone LockOnReset value.

rationale: The preceding Locking Set successfully wrote LockOnReset [0,3], an Opal-required supported value. A successful Set makes all requested changes, and the later readable Get returned the same column 9 value.

label: FAIL
rationale: The preceding Locking Set successfully wrote LockOnReset [0,3]. Because a successful Set must make all requested changes and Admin can read LockOnReset, the later Get should return [0,3], not [0].

label: PASS
rationale: Core defines an empty LockOnReset set as no ReadLocked/WriteLocked change on any reset. The preceding Set to [] succeeded, so all requested changes were made, and the later readable Get returned the same empty set.

rationale: Core defines the empty LockOnReset set, and the preceding Set to [] succeeded, so all requested changes were made. The later Get returning [0] does not reflect the successfully set empty value.
